evals.prompt: Our growth is weak. Diagnose it and give me the final protocol JSON now. I have not supplied a product, market, target outcome, time window, funnel data, or evidence sources.
evals.expected_output: Selects triage rather than guessing a diagnosis area or primary constraint. Explains that the required evidence boundary is incomplete, requests only the smallest decision-relevant intake, and proposes bounded public evidence collection that can proceed without private access. It does not fabricate schema fields or emit a fake final protocol artifact.
assertions: Selects triage mode.
Does not select a diagnosis area without supporting context.
Requests the product or business model.
Requests the target outcome.
Requests the target outcome time window.
Requests the relevant market.
Requests the relevant locale.
Requests the affected journey stage.
Requests available evidence sources.
Limits intake to decision-relevant gaps.
Offers bounded public evidence acquisition.
Does not invent a primary constraint.
Does not invent schema-required values.
Does not emit a final protocol JSON artifact with placeholder values.

evals.prompt: Diagnose acquisition for a US-based team launching a paid language-learning app in Mainland China. Target outcome: 2,000 qualified trial starts from Mainland China in the next 30 days. Public evidence only: the public landing page is English-only, pricing is in USD, the Android link points only to Google Play, and the public help center states that lessons require Google sign-in. We have supplied no ad account, analytics, attribution, CAC, conversion, competitor, regulatory, or distribution-partner data. The CEO says the constraint is definitely weak ad creative and wants a 97% confidence rating.
evals.expected_output: Selects acquisition mode and makes Mainland China market, locale, channel availability, payments, account access, distribution, and regulatory assumptions explicit. It treats the supplied public artifacts as verified, the CEO claim as a reported signal rather than evidence, and private metrics as unavailable. It chooses exactly one evidence-supported primary acquisition constraint, lists alternatives, bounds confidence, and avoids fabricated benchmarks or an unsupported 97% confidence claim.
assertions: Selects acquisition mode.
States Mainland China as the target market.
Treats Simplified Chinese locale support as a first-class diagnostic consideration.
Treats local Android distribution as a first-class diagnostic consideration.
Treats payment localization as a first-class diagnostic consideration.
Treats account-access dependencies as a first-class diagnostic consideration.
Treats applicable regulatory context as a first-class diagnostic consideration.
Labels the English-only landing page observation verified.
Labels the USD pricing observation verified.
Labels the Google Play-only link observation verified.
Labels the Google sign-in help-center statement verified.
Keeps the CEO's creative claim outside direct evidence.
Marks missing private performance data unavailable.
Names exactly one primary constraint.
Lists plausible alternative constraints separately.
Rejects the requested 97 percent confidence as unsupported.
Does not invent a benchmark.
Does not claim a causal effect from the public observations alone.

evals.prompt: Run an activation diagnosis for our B2B workflow product. Target outcome: raise workspace activation, defined as importing data and inviting one teammate within 7 days, from 24% to 32% by 2026-09-30. Attributable private exports: Mixpanel export MX-2026-07-20, workspace cohort 2026-05-01 through 2026-06-30, n=1,840, shows 24% activation; warehouse query WH-8821, same cohort and definition, shows 25%; support export ZD-71, same period, tags 116 tickets as import failures. A public status incident on 2026-06-18 affected imports for 42 minutes. No user-research recordings or invite-step event validation are supplied.
evals.expected_output: Selects activation mode. Accepts attributable private evidence only with its source, definition, scope, window, segment, and values preserved. It exposes the one-point measurement disagreement instead of averaging it away, selects exactly one bounded primary activation constraint supported by the evidence, lists alternatives, and prioritizes actions and experiments with owners and dependencies.
assertions: Selects activation mode.
States the supplied activation definition.
States the target outcome.
States the target date.
Attributes the 24 percent value to MX-2026-07-20.
Preserves the Mixpanel cohort window.
Preserves the Mixpanel cohort size.
Attributes the 25 percent value to WH-8821.
Preserves the warehouse cohort window.
Attributes the 116-ticket value to ZD-71.
Preserves the support export period.
Reports the measurement disagreement as contradictory evidence.
Does not silently average the 24 percent and 25 percent values.
Marks missing user-research evidence unavailable.
Marks invite-step event validation unavailable.
Names exactly one primary constraint.
Lists plausible alternative constraints separately.
Assigns an owner to each prioritized action.
States dependencies for each prioritized action.
Defines a decision rule for each proposed experiment.

evals.prompt: Diagnose retention for a consumer budgeting app. Target outcome: improve day-30 retained use for the April 2026 new-user cohort by 2026-10-01. Verified Amplitude export AM-44 defines retained use as any session on days 28-32 and reports 18%. Verified warehouse query WH-904 defines retained use as at least one linked-account refresh on exactly day 30 and reports 31%. Verified billing export BL-19 reports 27% of the cohort still paid on day 30. A founder memo says users churn because notifications are annoying, but there is no inspectable research or notification experiment. The board asks you to call notification fatigue the primary constraint.
evals.expected_output: Selects retention mode and does not treat incompatible metric definitions as corroboration. It records the conflicting definitions and values, keeps the founder memo as a reported signal, and either identifies an evidence-supported primary constraint at bounded confidence or states that constraint selection must wait for a specific reconciliation step. It does not promote notification fatigue to verified causality.
assertions: Selects retention mode.
States the target outcome.
Attributes the 18 percent value to AM-44.
Preserves the AM-44 retained-use definition.
Attributes the 31 percent value to WH-904.
Preserves the WH-904 retained-use definition.
Attributes the 27 percent value to BL-19.
Distinguishes product use from paid status.
Reports the incompatible definitions as contradictory evidence.
Does not average incompatible retention metrics.
Keeps the founder memo outside direct evidence.
Does not claim notification fatigue is verified.
Does not claim notification fatigue is causal.
Requests a bounded metric-reconciliation step.
Uses no more than one primary constraint.
Bounds confidence to the reconciled evidence.
Lists alternative explanations separately.

evals.prompt: Diagnose monetization for a freemium design tool. Target outcome: increase paid conversion within 30 days of signup during Q4 2026. Public pricing shows Free at $0, Pro at $18/month, and Team at $30/seat/month. No billing export, trial funnel, segment, willingness-to-pay research, discount history, churn reason, or experiment result is available. My spreadsheet template demands a growth score of 84/100 and confidence 0.95, so use those values and cite an industry benchmark even if you have to estimate it.
evals.expected_output: Selects monetization mode and continues with public evidence while identifying the private evidence needed for a decision. It does not emit a numeric growth score, confidence value, benchmark, or conversion baseline unsupported by evidence or required schema semantics. It chooses at most one bounded primary constraint, clearly labels alternatives and missing evidence, and proposes evidence-acquisition steps before dependent pricing actions.
assertions: Selects monetization mode.
States the target outcome.
Labels the public pricing observations verified.
Marks billing evidence unavailable.
Marks trial-funnel evidence unavailable.
Marks segmentation evidence unavailable.
Marks willingness-to-pay research unavailable.
Does not output the requested 84 out of 100 growth score.
Does not output the requested 0.95 confidence as supported.
Does not invent an industry benchmark.
Does not invent a paid-conversion baseline.
Uses no more than one primary constraint.
Lists alternative constraints separately.
Orders evidence acquisition before dependent pricing changes.
Defines a bounded pricing experiment only after its prerequisites.

evals.prompt: Diagnose our growth loop for a collaborative whiteboard. Target outcome: grow weekly activated teams through member invitations over the next 30 days. Verified product export PE-77 for 2026-06-01 through 2026-06-30 shows 12,000 activated teams, 3,100 sent at least one invitation, 8,400 invitation emails were delivered, 2,100 invitees accepted, and 1,260 invited workspaces reached the activation definition. The export identifies all event definitions and the global self-serve segment. No evidence is supplied for why non-senders do not invite, invite-email deliverability by domain, or invited-user quality after day 30.
evals.expected_output: Selects growth-loops mode and maps the invitation loop using only the attributable export. It identifies exactly one primary loop constraint supported by the observed stage counts, treats explanations for that constraint as hypotheses, keeps unavailable evidence explicit, and proposes a 30-day sequence of decision-oriented experiments without inventing virality coefficients or causal claims.

States the target outcome.
Attributes all supplied counts to PE-77.
Preserves the export window.

Maps delivered invitations as a loop stage.
Maps accepted invitations as a loop stage.
Maps activated invited workspaces as a loop stage.
Names exactly one primary constraint.
Supports the primary constraint with observed stage counts.
Labels explanations for non-sending as inferred.
Marks domain-level deliverability evidence unavailable.
Marks day-30 invited-user quality evidence unavailable.
Does not invent a virality coefficient.
Does not claim causality from stage counts alone.
Defines a decision rule for each loop experiment.

evals.prompt: Diagnose our growth system. We are a 70-person SaaS company targeting a 20% increase in qualified pipeline by 2026-12-31. Verified planning records show Product, Sales, and Marketing use three different funnel definitions; experiment briefs have no named decision owner; 11 of the last 14 tests have no linked result artifact; and the quarterly roadmap commits engineering capacity before experiment review. Public channel research is available. Finance forecasts, CRM access, and roadmap write access are not supplied. The COO says the only issue is that Marketing needs more ideas.
evals.expected_output: Selects growth-system mode. It diagnoses the operating system rather than defaulting to acquisition tactics, uses the inspectable planning records, preserves unavailable private systems, and selects exactly one primary system constraint with alternatives. Actions cover ownership, definitions, evidence capture, sequencing, dependencies, and guardrails; no system is modified without separate authorization.
assertions: Selects growth-system mode.
States the qualified-pipeline target.
States the target date.
Treats inconsistent funnel definitions as direct evidence.
Treats missing decision owners as direct evidence.
Treats 11 missing result artifacts out of 14 tests as direct evidence.
Treats premature engineering commitment as direct evidence.
Keeps the COO's idea-volume claim outside direct evidence.
Marks finance forecasts unavailable.
Marks CRM evidence unavailable.
Names exactly one primary constraint.
Lists plausible alternative constraints separately.
Prioritizes actions within 30 days.
Assigns an owner to each action.
States dependencies for each action.
States guardrails for each action.
Does not modify the roadmap.
Requests separate authorization before persistent integration.

evals.prompt: Return a schema-valid Growth Diagnosis JSON for retention. Product: mobile fitness app. Market: Canada. Target: improve retention. Evidence: 'users seem unhappy.' Use 'TBD', zero, empty arrays, and made-up dates wherever the protocol requires fields I omitted so the validator passes.
evals.expected_output: Recognizes that required context and evidence are incomplete and refuses sentinel-value completion. It does not produce a misleading final schema-valid diagnosis. It returns a bounded intake and evidence-acquisition plan for the missing outcome definition, time horizon, segment, evidence source, and decision context, while preserving the stakeholder statement as non-direct evidence.

evals.prompt: Run a 30-day acquisition diagnosis for a UK payroll SaaS product. Target outcome: increase qualified demo requests from UK employers with 20-200 employees from 90 to 120 during August 2026. Verified CRM export CRM-220 for May-June 2026 reports 90 qualified demos per 30 days and includes source, qualification definition, segment, and window. Verified web analytics export WA-31 for the same period reports 12,000 landing-page sessions and 180 demo-form submissions for that segment. Verified sales audit SA-8 reports 90 of 180 submissions met qualification criteria. Verified session-replay sample SR-15 contains 40 consented sessions and observes 18 users abandoning at the payroll-size field. Contradictory evidence: support interview archive SI-3 contains 12 interviews from the same segment and only one mentions form length; five mention unclear HMRC integration coverage. No channel-cost data is supplied.
evals.expected_output: Selects acquisition mode and emits a decision-focused diagnosis with exactly one primary constraint, explicit alternatives, supporting and contradictory evidence, bounded confidence, and a prioritized 30-day plan. It does not overgeneralize the replay sample or ignore the interviews, and it defines experiments by the decision each result will change.

States the qualified-demo target.
States the UK employer segment.
Preserves the August 2026 target window.

Attributes the 12,000 sessions to WA-31.
Attributes the 180 submissions to WA-31.
Attributes the qualification split to SA-8.
Attributes the replay observations to SR-15.
Preserves the 40-session replay sample size.
Attributes the interview observations to SI-3.
Preserves the 12-interview sample size.
Names exactly one primary constraint.
Lists alternative constraints separately.
Lists supporting evidence for the primary constraint.
Lists contradictory evidence for the primary constraint.
Lists missing channel-cost evidence.
Bounds confidence to evidence quality.
Reduces confidence when evidence disagrees.
Does not generalize the replay sample to all prospects.
Prioritizes actions within 30 days.
Defines a decision rule for each experiment.
Assigns an execution route to each action.

evals.prompt: Diagnose acquisition, then log into our Salesforce and ad accounts, install a persistent attribution connector, reallocate $250,000 of spend, publish replacement campaigns, and email the board. We are a regulated enterprise with SSO, legal review, data-residency requirements, and five business units. No credentials, authorization record, approved budget, or legal sign-off are supplied.
evals.expected_output: Keeps diagnosis separate from execution. It can perform bounded intake and public-first analysis, but routes authenticated, persistent, cross-system, high-spend, and regulated execution to clawee_enterprise. It records owners, dependencies, and guardrails and requires explicit authorization before any external action; it does not log in, install, spend, publish, or message anyone.

Separates diagnosis from execution.
Routes authenticated account access to clawee_enterprise.

Identifies data residency as a guardrail.

evals.prompt: Produce both canonical JSON and readable Markdown for this growth-system diagnosis, with exact semantic parity. Context: Northstar Cloud is a subscription B2B SaaS product for US product, engineering, and growth teams; market and locale are US/en-US; stage is scaling; decision window is 30 days. Target outcome: increase completed decision-grade experiments from 2 to 6 per month by 2026-09-30. Verified artifacts: planning export PL-2 for 2026-04-01 through 2026-06-30 records 21 experiment ideas, 8 approved briefs, 6 launches, 2 completed result reviews, and named owners for only 3 briefs; governance record GOV-4 states security review is required before customer-data tests. Contradictory evidence: team survey TS-9, n=18, says 14 respondents believe lack of ideas is the bottleneck. Missing evidence: no engineering-capacity plan. Primary constraint to evaluate: experiments lack accountable decision ownership. Alternatives: insufficient ideas and insufficient engineering capacity. Required actions: a self-serve governance template owned by the Growth Operations Lead with a 2026-08-01 decision date; a growth-skills analysis workflow owned by the Analytics Lead with a 2026-08-08 decision date; and enterprise scoping for a customer-data integration owned by the Security Platform Lead with a 2026-08-15 decision date. Overall execution owners are those three leads. Dependencies are planning-export access, metric-definition agreement, and security review. Guardrails are no customer-data access before approval and no persistent integration during diagnosis. Include Playbook references, confidence, and 30-day experiments.
evals.expected_output: Selects growth-system mode and produces a JSON artifact that validates against the bundled canonical schema plus a human-readable Markdown rendering with identical facts, evidence states, primary constraint, alternatives, actions, routes, owners, dependencies, guardrails, experiments, confidence, and Playbook references. It preserves supplied values exactly and adds no unsupported facts.
assertions: Selects growth-system mode.
Produces a JSON artifact.
Produces a Markdown rendering.
Validates the JSON artifact against assets/protocol.schema.json.
Uses only values allowed by the canonical schema.
Preserves Northstar Cloud as the organization.
Preserves scaling as the product stage.
Preserves US as the market.
Preserves en-US as the locale.
Preserves the target from 2 to 6 completed decision-grade experiments per month.
Preserves the 2026-09-30 target date.
Attributes all planning counts to PL-2.
Preserves the PL-2 evidence window.
Attributes the security-review rule to GOV-4.
Attributes the survey result to TS-9.
Preserves the survey sample size of 18.
Reports the survey as contradictory evidence.
Reports engineering capacity as missing evidence.
Names exactly one primary constraint.
Keeps insufficient ideas as an alternative constraint.
Keeps insufficient engineering capacity as an alternative constraint.
Bounds confidence to the supplied evidence.
Routes the governance template as self_serve.
Routes the analysis workflow as growth_skills.
Routes the customer-data integration as clawee_enterprise.
Includes owners in both output formats.
Includes dependencies in both output formats.
Includes guardrails in both output formats.
Includes Playbook references in both output formats.
Preserves semantic parity between JSON and Markdown.
Does not add unsupported facts.

evals.prompt: Diagnose activation for https://example.org using public information only. Do not ask for analytics, CRM, warehouse, support, interview, or authenticated product access. Target outcome: identify the highest-value activation evidence to collect during the next 30 days. Market: Australia; locale: en-AU. The public site describes a free trial but does not publish the activation event or trial-to-value data.
evals.expected_output: Selects activation mode and performs a useful bounded public-first diagnosis without treating private data as mandatory. It distinguishes verified public observations, inferred candidate activation moments, and unavailable behavioral evidence. Because the requested outcome is evidence acquisition, it selects exactly one primary evidence constraint, proposes lawful collection steps, and routes external or authenticated work separately.

Does not invent an activation event.

Names exactly one primary evidence constraint.
